mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions, producing negative 5-day returns when INDPRO rises, or fails to maintain positive predictive accuracy in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in the consumer price index erode real corporate profit margins and trigger expectations of tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns, or the negative correlation breaks down during periods of strong nominal GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50,000 signals abrupt labor market contraction and reduced aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50k in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months following a PAYEMS drop > 50k, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth occurring alongside a federal funds rate below 0.5% creates an optimal liquidity and growth environment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% despite positive GDP growth, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates efficient capacity utilization and labor market tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails when UNRATE remains flat or rises despite INDPRO growth, or predictive accuracy drops below 52% during stagflationary periods.
